# brb-traveling

> A personal travel journal and photo diary — field notes, tips, and photos from Iceland, Finland, Norway, Taiwan, China, and Korea.

A personal travel blog by Daniel Choi. Each destination has a written travel journal entry
with practical tips and a photo gallery.

## Pages

- Home: https://www.brb-traveling.com
- Travel journal (all posts): https://www.brb-traveling.com/blog
- RSS feed: https://www.brb-traveling.com/rss.xml
- Sitemap: https://www.brb-traveling.com/sitemap.xml

## Destinations

### China (May 10, 2025)

Beijing duck, Chengdu hotpot, the Great Wall, and a country that runs on one app — the biggest culture shift in the shortest amount of time.

- Blog post: https://www.brb-traveling.com/blog/china
- Photo gallery: https://www.brb-traveling.com/photos/china
- Quick tips:
  - Best time to go: April–May or September–October for mild weather; avoid Golden Week holidays when everything is packed.
  - Getting around: Set up WeChat Pay or Alipay before you land — it unlocks everything. High-speed rail beats flying between most cities.
  - Before you land: Download a working VPN and offline translation packs; most western apps and maps are blocked once you're there.

### Taiwan (March 15, 2025)

A last-minute week in Taipei that turned into the warmest stop of the year — midnight beef noodle soup, Elephant Mountain, a monsoon, and the kindest strangers anywhere.

- Blog post: https://www.brb-traveling.com/blog/taiwan
- Photo gallery: https://www.brb-traveling.com/photos/taiwan
- Quick tips:
  - Best time to go: October–April for cooler weather; summer is hot, humid, and monsoon-prone (pack a poncho either way).
  - Getting around: The Taipei MRT is spotless and easy; grab an EasyCard on arrival. The high-speed rail makes Tainan and Kaohsiung easy day-or-two trips.
  - Must-eats: Beef noodle soup, xiao long bao at Din Tai Fung (or any local spot), night market everything, and bubble tea from the source.

### Korea (March 8, 2025)

Twenty-two hours in Seoul, treated like a sprint — KBBQ, a jjimjilbang, the Han River at night, and showing two friends the city my family is from.

- Blog post: https://www.brb-traveling.com/blog/korea
- Photo gallery: https://www.brb-traveling.com/photos/korea
- Quick tips:
  - Best time to go: April for cherry blossoms or October for fall color; summers are humid and late July–August is monsoon season.
  - Getting around: Get a T-money card and use Naver Map or Kakao Map — Google Maps barely works for walking directions in Korea.
  - Must-eats: KBBQ, late-night pojangmacha street tents, Gwangjang Market bindaetteok, and a convenience-store haul at least once.

### Finland (December 14, 2024)

I went to Finland for the SkySauna and Santa Claus Village, and stayed for the quiet — Helsinki saunas, Arctic Circle stamps, and a husky sled through complete silence.

- Blog post: https://www.brb-traveling.com/blog/finland
- Photo gallery: https://www.brb-traveling.com/photos/finland
- Quick tips:
  - Best time to go: December–February for the full Arctic winter (Santa Claus Village, husky sleds); summer for lake-and-sauna season.
  - Getting around: Helsinki is walkable with great trams; take the overnight train or a short flight up to Rovaniemi for Lapland.
  - Must-dos: A public sauna (Löyly or Allas), the SkySauna ferris wheel, and crossing the Arctic Circle with snowpants you don't mind getting wet.

### Norway (July 20, 2024)

Oslo, Bergen, and a fjord cruise that looks fake in person — plus a wordless gym session that proved the deadlift is a universal language.

- Blog post: https://www.brb-traveling.com/blog/norway
- Photo gallery: https://www.brb-traveling.com/photos/norway
- Quick tips:
  - Best time to go: May–September for long days and calm fjord cruises; winter works if you're chasing snow and northern lights instead.
  - Getting around: The Oslo–Bergen railway is one of the world's great train rides. Bergen itself is walkable; book fjord cruises ahead in summer.
  - Must-dos: The Nærøyfjord cruise (yes, it's touristy — it's still the trip), the Fløyen funicular, and the Viking ship museum in Oslo.

### Iceland (June 8, 2024)

Driving Iceland's south coast, where the scenery resets every twenty minutes — black sand beaches, glaciers, hot springs, and the best gas station food on the planet.

- Blog post: https://www.brb-traveling.com/blog/iceland
- Photo gallery: https://www.brb-traveling.com/photos/iceland
- Quick tips:
  - Best time to go: June–August for endless daylight and open roads; September for fewer crowds and a shot at the northern lights.
  - Getting around: Rent a car — the south coast and ring road are the whole point. Watch the wind warnings and fuel up whenever you can.
  - Must-eats: Reykjavík hot dogs, lamb soup, and anything from an N1 gas station — genuinely better than most sit-down restaurants.

## Travel guides

### Iceland's south coast: a 3-day road trip guide (May 1, 2026)

A practical 3-day route from Reykjavík to the glacier lagoon — the waterfalls and black sand beaches worth stopping for, what driving in Iceland is actually like, and what to pack.

- Guide: https://www.brb-traveling.com/guides/iceland-south-coast-road-trip
- Related trip: Iceland — https://www.brb-traveling.com/blog/iceland
- Quick tips:
  - Rental car: 2WD is enough in summer; add gravel and door insurance, and check road.is plus the wind forecast every morning.
  - Where to stay: Split the nights between Vík and the Höfn/Jökulsárlón area so no driving day is longer than three hours.
  - Worth booking ahead: The Jökulsárlón zodiac boat tour sells out in summer; glacier hikes and ice caves need a guide regardless of season.

### 22 hours in Seoul: a layover itinerary (May 1, 2026)

How to turn a long Incheon layover into a real night in Seoul — KBBQ, a jjimjilbang instead of a hotel, the Han River at night, and breakfast at Gwangjang Market.

- Guide: https://www.brb-traveling.com/guides/seoul-layover-itinerary
- Related trip: Korea — https://www.brb-traveling.com/blog/korea
- Quick tips:
  - Getting into the city: AREX train from Incheon to Seoul Station (~1 hour), pay with a T-money card, stash big bags in a station locker.
  - Where to sleep: Skip the hotel — a jjimjilbang (Korean bathhouse) covers a shower, sauna, and a few hours of sleep for a fraction of the price.
  - Don't land without: Naver Map or Kakao Map installed, a T-money card, and a rough plan for one neighborhood — not five.
